dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "When," asked one of the women, "will we enjoy the death throes of the red one? or does Lorquas Ptomel, Jed, intend holding her for ransom?"

speaker.notes: Context suggests a female speaker, but the source wording is still ambiguous.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: They have decided to carry her with us back to Thark, and exhibit her last agonies at the great games before Tal Hajus,
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "They have decided to carry her with us back to Thark, and exhibit her last agonies at the great games before Tal Hajus," replied Sarkoja.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: What will be the manner of her going out?
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "What will be the manner of her going out?" inquired Sola. "She is very small and very beautiful; I had hoped that they would hold her for ransom."

speaker.notes: Context suggests a female speaker, but the source wording is still ambiguous.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: when all the hollows of the land were filled with water, and the peoples were as soft as the stuff they sailed upon. In our day we have progressed to a point where such sentiments mark weakness and atavism. It will not be well for you to permit Tars Tarkas to learn that you hold such degenerate sentiments, as I doubt that he would care to entrust such as you with the grave responsibilities of maternity.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: I see nothing wrong with my expression of interest in this red woman,
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "I see nothing wrong with my expression of interest in this red woman," retorted Sola. "She has never harmed us, nor would she should we have fallen into her hands. It is only the men of her kind who war upon us, and I have ever thought...

delivery_source.dialogue_text: I see nothing wrong with my expression of interest in this red woman,
delivery_source.source_excerpt: "I see nothing wrong with my expression of interest in this red woman," retorted Sola. "She has never harmed us, nor would she should we have fallen into her hands. It is only the men of her kind who war upon us, and I have ever thought...

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: She has never harmed us, nor would she should we have fallen into her hands. It is only the men of her kind who war upon us, and I have ever thought that their attitude toward us is but the reflection of ours toward them. They live at peace with all their fellows, except when duty calls upon them to make war, while we are at peace with none; forever warring among our own kind as well as upon the red men, and even in our own communities the individuals fight amongst themselves. Oh, it is one continual, awful period of bloodshed from the time we break the shell until we gladly embrace the bosom of the river of mystery, the dark and ancient Iss which carries us to an unknown, but at least no more frightful and terrible existence! Fortunate indeed is he who meets his end in an early death. Say what you please to Tars Tarkas, he can mete out no worse fate to me than a continuation of the horrible existence we are forced to lead in this life.
